projects.title: Unit 9 Project — Coordinate Plane & Integers in Action
projects.desc: Apply graphing, integers, and the coordinate plane to a real-world capstone challenge for Unit 9.

projects.title: Fraction & Decimal Grid Architect
projects.desc: Place and read fractions and decimals as rational numbers on a grid (6.NS.6).

projects.title: Number Line Jumper
projects.desc: Locate and order rational numbers along the number line (6.NS.7).

title: Rational Numbers on the Number Line
contentObjective: I can place rational numbers, including fractions and decimals, on a number line.
languageObjective: I can explain my reasoning using the words rational number, fraction, decimal, and number line.

turnAndTalk.question: How can a fraction like 1/2, a decimal like 0.75, and an integer like -3 all be rational numbers?
turnAndTalk.kernel: All of these are rational numbers because each one can be ___.

stems.en: A rational number is any number you can write as ___.
stems.es: Un numero racional es cualquier numero que puedes escribir como ___.

stems.en: For example, -3 can be written as ___.
stems.es: Por ejemplo, -3 se puede escribir como ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student explains a rational number is any number that can be written as a fraction, including integers and decimals.
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to explain how to place 1/2, 0.75, and -3 on the same number line.
extendStems: To place 0.75 on the number line, I would ___.
I can compare a fraction and a decimal by ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Where would you place -1 1/2 on the number line, and how do you decide between which two integers it falls?
turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student places -1 1/2 between -1 and -2, halfway, reasoning from the value of the fraction part.
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to compare -1 1/2 and -1 using the number line and justify which is greater.
extendStems: On the number line, ___ is to the right of ___.

turnAndTalk.question: How can rewriting a fraction as a decimal (or a decimal as a fraction) help you compare two rational numbers?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Rewriting the numbers in the same form helps because ___.

stems.en: If I turn 3/4 into a decimal, I get ___.
Now I can compare them because ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student explains converting to a common form (both decimals or both fractions) makes comparison and ordering easier.
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to generalize a strategy for ordering a mixed list of fractions, decimals, and integers.
extendStems: My strategy for ordering a mixed list is ___.
Putting them all in the same form helps because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Is every integer a rational number? Is every rational number an integer? Explain your thinking.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Every integer is a rational number, but ___.

stems.en: Every integer is rational because it can be written as ___.
stems.es: Todo entero es racional porque se puede escribir como ___.

stems.en: But not every rational number is an integer because ___.
stems.es: Pero no todo numero racional es un entero porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student explains integers are rational (write as a fraction over 1) but rationals like 1/2 are not integers.
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to critique the statement 'all fractions are integers' with an example.
extendStems: An example that disproves it is ___.

vocabulary.definition: Any number you can write as a fraction. This includes fractions, decimals, and integers.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Cualquier número que puedes escribir como fracción. Incluye fracciones, decimales y enteros.
vocabulary.cloze: Any number that can be written as a fraction, including integers and decimals, is a ___.

examples.why: It is written as a fraction.

vocabulary.definition: A number that shows part of a whole, like 3/4.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número que muestra una parte de un todo, como 3/4.
vocabulary.visual: 3/4 means 3 out of 4 equal parts — like 3 slices of a pizza cut into 4
vocabulary.cloze: A number that shows part of a whole, like 3/5, is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A number with a dot, like 0.5, that shows a part less than one.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número con un punto, como 0.5, que muestra una parte menor que uno.
vocabulary.visual: 0.5 = 1/2 (halfway between 0 and 1 on the number line)
vocabulary.cloze: A number that uses a point to show parts of a whole is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A straight line with numbers spaced out evenly.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una línea recta con números separados de forma pareja.
vocabulary.cloze: A line with numbers in order used to place and compare values is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: Having the same value, just written a different way.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Tener el mismo valor, escrito de otra forma.
vocabulary.visual: 1/2 = 0.5 = 2/4 — all name the same point on the number line
vocabulary.cloze: Numbers that name the same value, like 1/2 and 0.5, are ___.

vocabulary.definition: Whole numbers and their opposites, like -2, -1, 0, 1, 2.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Números enteros y sus opuestos, como -2, -1, 0, 1, 2.
vocabulary.cloze: A whole number or its opposite is an ___.

launch.narrative: Captain Vega's advanced treasure map uses precise coordinates — not just whole numbers! One clue says the treasure is at -2.5 on the map (halfway between -2 and -3). Another clue points to 3/4 of the way from 0 to 1. A third clue leads to -1 1/2. To find each treasure, you must plot these exact rational numbers on the number line.
launch.contextImage: Treasure map with a zoomed-in number line showing marks between whole numbers
noticePrompts: Where would -2.5 fall on a number line between -3 and -2?
How is plotting 3/4 different from plotting a whole number?
Can a negative number be a fraction or decimal?
wonderPrompts: How many rational numbers exist between any two integers?
Are there numbers that are NOT rational?

explore.instructions: Plot each rational number at its precise location on the number line.
explore.label: Plot each treasure clue at its exact location on the number line.

discourse.prompt: How did you decide exactly where to place rational numbers that fall between whole numbers?
discourse.sentenceFrame: To plot ___, I found the whole numbers it falls between, which are ___ and ___. Then I estimated ___ of the way between them because ___.

optional.stem: Where is -1.5 located on a number line?

optional.explanation: 3/4 = 0.75 is the largest of the four values.
The opposite of -2/3 is 2/3, the same distance on the other side of 0.

approaching.stem: Which point is located between -1 and -2 on the number line?
approaching.explanation: -1.5 is between -1 and -2 because it is 0.5 units to the left of -1 (or 0.5 units to the right of -2).

approaching.label: Match each fraction to its decimal equivalent.

approaching.stem: Where is -3/4 located on the number line?
approaching.explanation: -3/4 = -0.75, which is between 0 and -1. It is 3/4 of the way from 0 toward -1.

onLevel.instructions: Convert between fraction and decimal form. Then plot on a mental number line to compare.

onLevel.label: Order these rational numbers from LEAST to GREATEST.

onLevel.stem: Which rational number is closest to 0 on the number line?
onLevel.explanation: |-1/4| = 0.25, |0.5| = 0.5, |-2| = 2, |1.75| = 1.75. Since 0.25 is the smallest absolute value, -1/4 is closest to zero.

extending.title: Find the Plotting Error

workedExample.label: Student thinking
workedExample.work: -3/4 has a 3 and a 4, so I go to -3 on the number line and then count 4 more to the left

extending.correctWork: -3/4 means negative three-fourths, which is 3/4 of the way from 0 toward -1. It should be plotted between 0 and -1, not at -7. The student treated the numerator and denominator as separate whole numbers.
hints: What does the fraction 3/4 actually mean? How much of a whole is it?
-3/4 is a number between 0 and -1. It is 3/4 of one unit to the left of zero.

extending.prompt: Name three different rational numbers between -1 and 0. Write each as both a fraction and a decimal. Explain how you know they are between -1 and 0.
extending.sentenceFrame: Three rational numbers between -1 and 0 are: ___ = ___, ___ = ___, and ___ = ___. I know they are between -1 and 0 because ___.

extending.label: Which side is greater? Compare -2/3 and -3/4.
extending.instructions: Convert to decimals: -2/3 ≈ -0.667 and -3/4 = -0.75. Since -0.667 is closer to zero, -2/3 > -3/4. The left side is greater.

connect.scenario: A scientist measures the water level of a lake relative to normal level (0). Monday: +0.75 ft, Tuesday: -1.25 ft, Wednesday: -0.5 ft, Thursday: +1.5 ft. She needs to plot these on a graph to track changes.
connect.promptQuestion: How do rational numbers help the scientist describe water levels more precisely than integers alone?
connect.prompt: Rational numbers help because ___. For example, -1.25 ft means the water is ___ below normal, which is between ___ and ___ on the number line. Without fractions and decimals, the scientist could only say ___.

exitTicket.stem: Which rational number is located between -2 and -3 on the number line?
exitTicket.explanation: -2.75 is between -2 and -3 because it is 0.75 units to the left of -2. -1.5 is between -1 and -2, and -3.5 is between -3 and -4.
